Rhamnus cathartica, also known as buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with a history of traditional use in herbal medicine. The health benefits of buckthorn include its potential to support digestive health, as it is traditionally used as a laxative and to treat constipation, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhamnus cathartica include anthraquinones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with buckthorn include tinctures, capsules, and teas, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of constipation.
